query: How many hearts does an octopus have, and where do most of its neurons live?

pages.title: Octopuses
pages.body: Octopuses are cephalopods with three hearts and blue copper-based blood. About two thirds of their neurons live in their arms, not the central brain. See [[Cephalopoda]].
facts: Two thirds of neurons in arms

pages.title: Cephalopoda
pages.body: Class of molluscs including octopuses, squid, and cuttlefish.

query: Why do long-running transactions in Postgres cause table bloat?

pages.title: MVCC
pages.body: Multi-Version Concurrency Control creates a new tuple for every update; old versions wait for [[VACUUM]].

pages.title: VACUUM
pages.body: VACUUM reclaims space from dead tuples. It cannot remove tuples still visible to any open transaction, so long-running transactions block cleanup and produce table bloat.
facts: Cannot remove tuples visible to any open txn

query: In what year did Julius Caesar cross the Rubicon, and what was the political consequence?

pages.title: Julius Caesar
pages.body: Roman general and statesman. Crossed the [[Rubicon]] in 49 BCE, an act of war against the Senate.

pages.title: Rubicon
pages.body: Small river in northern Italy. Crossing it with an army was illegal under Roman law and was effectively a declaration of war on the Senate.

query: What is the population of Tokyo in 2024?

pages.title: Octopuses
pages.body: Octopuses are cephalopods with three hearts.

query: Where exactly in the chloroplast do the light-dependent reactions take place, and what do they produce?

pages.title: Photosynthesis
pages.body: Process that converts light, CO2, and water into glucose and O2. Light-dependent reactions take place in the [[Thylakoid]] membranes; the [[Calvin cycle]] runs in the stroma.

pages.title: Thylakoid
pages.body: Membrane-bound compartment inside chloroplasts. The light-dependent reactions split water and produce ATP and NADPH here.
